nodes.label: AI as iterative test case expander
nodes.label_negative: Manual test case creation without AI
nodes.grounding_span: AI can be a very helpful tool in helping to iterate test data or even test cases once I have a first test or a template for it to work off of

nodes.label: AI as code analysis and error detection tool
nodes.grounding_span: It's often faster for the AI to analyse my code than for me to proofread it myself

nodes.label: AI as repetitive task automator
nodes.grounding_span: scenarios where I need to create a lot of repetitive test cases, or need a large amount of varied test data, which the AI can create easily

nodes.label: Maintaining personal expertise and project connection
nodes.label_negative: Over-reliance on AI leading to skill degradation
nodes.grounding_span: if I automate the process too much, I can risk becoming well connected to my project, which makes me worse at my job

nodes.label: AI handling of complex contextual tasks
nodes.label_negative: AI struggling with contextually dense scenarios
nodes.grounding_span: most of the AI models I have worked with have had difficulty working in complex context scenarios

nodes.label: Autonomous decision-making on AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I think it's best if I just make my own decisions, with maybe some input from management if I think it's necessary

nodes.label: Productivity and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: saves immense amounts of time, and takes a task that I hate off of my plate

nodes.label: Professional competence and expertise
nodes.grounding_span: I need to be challenging myself to be a subject matter expert, as well as just a more analytical and insightful engineer on my own

nodes.label: Quality of work output
nodes.grounding_span: increases my value as an employee as well

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for repetitive tasks
nodes.grounding_span: saves immense amounts of time, and takes a task that I hate off of my plate

nodes.label: Initial frustration with AI limitations
nodes.grounding_span: I was frustrated when first attempting to use AI as a resource for coding, and finding a lot of limitations due to quality of returns

nodes.label: Current acceptance and calibrated use
nodes.grounding_span: now that I understand the paramters better, I find I don't have frustrated feelings much at all

nodes.label: Deliberate avoidance of AI for initial design
nodes.grounding_span: I never use the AI to generate that for me

nodes.label: Iterative back-and-forth refinement
nodes.grounding_span: I will provide the test code to the AI, and request those iterations or other ideas, and see what the results are

nodes.label: Self-aware calibration of AI reliance
nodes.grounding_span: I try my best to be self-aware about how much I am relying on AI in my role in general
